name: Wonderful Millet Vegetable Balls
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons arrowroot powder (or substitute with up to 2 tablespoons unbleached flour or kamut)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add chopped onions and sauté until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
3. Add broccoli (or asparagus), carrots, mushrooms, celery (or bell pepper), olives, garlic, and vegetable bouillon cubes to the pan. Stir well.
4. Mix in tamari soy sauce (up to 3 tablespoons) or miso paste.
5. Add parsley (up to 3 tablespoons), dill weed, paprika, oregano, thyme, and sea salt if using.
6. Mix cooked millet, nut flour, and arrowroot powder together with the sautéed vegetables.
7. Form mixture into balls, about 1/8 to 1/4 cup each.
8. Bake at 400°F for 18-22 minutes until hot and toasted, or serve immediately as "meatballs" in a lightly oiled pan.
Serve with Arrowroot sauce, Mushroom Gravy, Vegetarian Gravy, or Toasted Cashew/Sesame Sauce. Incredibly delicious! MAKES: 2-4 dozen.
